title: Indian Geography and Rivers
description: A 50-question India geography round covering rivers, mountains, passes, coasts, lakes, plateaus, and landscapes.

questions.prompt: Which is the longest river that flows entirely within India?
questions.hint: It is often called the Dakshin Ganga.

questions.prompt: From which glacier does the Bhagirathi originate?
questions.hint: It is one of the best-known glaciers in Uttarakhand.

questions.prompt: The confluence at Devprayag gives rise to which river?
questions.hint: It is formed when the Alaknanda meets the Bhagirathi.

questions.prompt: Which major Indian river is called Tsangpo in Tibet?
questions.hint: It enters India through Arunachal Pradesh.

questions.prompt: Which west-flowing river runs in the rift valley between the Vindhya and Satpura ranges?
questions.hint: It empties into the Arabian Sea through the Gulf of Khambhat.

questions.prompt: Which river is often called the Dakshin Ganga?
questions.hint: It has one of the largest river basins in peninsular India.

questions.prompt: What is the highest peak in India?
questions.hint: It lies on the India-Nepal border.

questions.prompt: Which is the highest peak in the Western Ghats?
questions.hint: It is in Kerala.

questions.prompt: Which pass in Sikkim formed part of the old Silk Route to Tibet?
questions.hint: It begins with N.

questions.prompt: What is the only active volcano in India?
questions.hint: It lies in the Andaman Sea.

questions.prompt: Which is India's largest brackish water lagoon?
questions.hint: It lies on the Odisha coast.

questions.prompt: Which island group of India lies in the Arabian Sea?
questions.hint: It is made up mostly of coral atolls.

questions.prompt: Which cold desert valley lies in Himachal Pradesh?
questions.hint: Its name is often paired with Lahaul.

questions.prompt: On which river is the Hirakud Dam built?
questions.hint: It is one of the major rivers of eastern India.

questions.prompt: On which river is the Sardar Sarovar Dam built?
questions.hint: It is a major west-flowing river of India.

questions.prompt: On which river is the Tehri Dam built?
questions.hint: It later joins the Alaknanda to form the Ganga.

questions.prompt: What is the southernmost point of mainland India?
questions.hint: It is in Tamil Nadu.

questions.prompt: Which mountain range runs parallel to India's western coast?
questions.hint: It is a UNESCO biodiversity hotspot.

questions.prompt: Which major river originates near Mahabaleshwar?
questions.hint: It flows eastward across Maharashtra, Karnataka, Telangana, and Andhra Pradesh.

questions.prompt: On the banks of which river is Nashik located?
questions.hint: It is a sacred river of peninsular India.

questions.prompt: On the banks of which river is Ahmedabad located?
questions.hint: A major riverfront project is built along it.

questions.prompt: On the banks of which river is Guwahati located?
questions.hint: It is one of Asia's great transboundary rivers.

questions.prompt: On the banks of which river is Srinagar located?
questions.hint: It flows through Kashmir before entering Pakistan.

questions.prompt: Which plateau in eastern India is especially rich in minerals and coal?
questions.hint: Large parts of Jharkhand lie on it.

questions.prompt: Which river is often called the 'Sorrow of Bihar' because of its flooding?
questions.hint: It rises in Nepal before entering India.

questions.prompt: Which river was once called the 'Sorrow of Bengal' because of devastating floods?
questions.hint: It was later heavily dammed at DVC projects.

questions.prompt: Which river forms the famous Jog Falls in Karnataka?
questions.hint: It flows west to the Arabian Sea.

questions.prompt: Which gap in the Western Ghats connects Kerala and Tamil Nadu most directly?
questions.hint: It is an important low mountain pass for transport and monsoon winds.

questions.prompt: What is the largest river island in India?
questions.hint: It is associated with satras and neo-Vaishnav culture in Assam.

questions.prompt: Which lake in Manipur is famous for floating phumdis?
questions.hint: It is associated with the Keibul Lamjao National Park.

questions.prompt: Which lake in Rajasthan is India's largest inland salt lake?
questions.hint: It is an important centre for salt production.

questions.prompt: Which mountain pass lies on the highway between Srinagar and Leh?
questions.hint: It often closes in winter due to snow.

questions.prompt: What is the name of the great seasonal salt marsh in Gujarat?
questions.hint: It is divided into a Great and Little section.

questions.prompt: Which mountain range is considered one of the oldest fold mountains in the world and runs through western India?
questions.hint: Its highest peak is Guru Shikhar.

questions.prompt: Which river rises near Amarkantak and flows eastward to join the Ganga?
questions.hint: It is an important tributary entering from the south.

questions.prompt: Which west-flowing river empties into the Gulf of Khambhat south of the Narmada?
questions.hint: Surat lies near its mouth.

questions.prompt: Lakshadweep is built mainly from what kind of marine formation?
questions.hint: These structures are made by tiny sea organisms.

questions.prompt: What is the name of the west coast strip between Mumbai and Goa?
questions.hint: It gives its name to a railway line and a cuisine region.

questions.prompt: What is the name of the west coast strip between Goa and Mangaluru?
questions.hint: Its name is linked with Karnataka's coast.

questions.prompt: What is the name of the east coast strip along southeastern India between Andhra Pradesh and Tamil Nadu?
questions.hint: It was important in the colonial period.

questions.prompt: What is the highest peak of the Aravalli Range?
questions.hint: It is near Mount Abu.

questions.prompt: Which place in Madhya Pradesh is regarded as the source of the Narmada?
questions.hint: It is also associated with the source of the Son river.

questions.prompt: Which river forms the Dhuandhar Falls near Jabalpur?
questions.hint: It also cuts through the marble rocks at Bhedaghat.

questions.prompt: Which national park in Assam is best known for the one-horned rhinoceros and the Brahmaputra floodplain?
questions.hint: It is a UNESCO World Heritage Site.

questions.prompt: What is the name of the giant delta shared by the Ganga, Brahmaputra, and Meghna river systems?
questions.hint: It is also famous for mangrove forests and tigers.

questions.prompt: Which river flows through the marble gorge at Bhedaghat?
questions.hint: It is a major west-flowing river.

questions.prompt: Which major line of latitude passes through eight Indian states?
questions.hint: It is located at approximately 23.5 degrees north.

questions.prompt: In which union territory is Barren Island located?
questions.hint: It lies in the Bay of Bengal.

questions.prompt: Which mountain pass traditionally linked the Jammu region with the Kashmir Valley?
questions.hint: It now lends its name to a major tunnel corridor.

questions.prompt: Which river eventually disappears into the marshes of the Rann of Kutch rather than reaching the sea in a large estuary?
questions.hint: It flows across Rajasthan.
